item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: New image added, originally taken by Edmundo Saez and licensed under CC Attribution-Share Alike 4.0: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bunker_under_the_Milky_Way.jpg.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image sourced is under the CC BY-NC-SA 2.0 license and is therefore not compliant with the SCP Wiki's CC-BY-SA 3.0 license. As such, the image has been removed. Make sure to follow our image use policy when picking any future images to make sure the license is compatible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Too bad the Bronze Age civilization didn't realize they were sending their dead into what is essentially Space Hell.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damn, this article went straight from "We are all made of star stuff" to "You'll float too" in the span of two sentences. Fucking Christ man. I was initially trying to decide whether they feel eternal heat or eternal cold, but then I realized it doesn't matter. Extreme temperatures end up overloading our senses to the point that they feel nearly identical. If I put out a lit cigar onto one hand and pushed a cotton swab of liquid nitrogen on the other, the sensations would be indistinguishable. It all hurts, really fucking bad. Damn. +1 you monster.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Their thermoreceptors are going o- wait, holy shiiiiiiiiiiii
This alone was good enough for a +1

item.title: Re: +101!!!
item.content:encoded: Happy that you liked the observation. Great job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Initially downvoted. Came back to re-rate.

It's okay, could be spookier. The formula here just seems rote. I think most of the Short SCP contest entries could use more detail.
Your upvote is well-placed. And your suggestion is an awesome one. A literal star mind, incubating, burning, learning…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did anyone else think each new orb was forming a sort of node and that overall a complex organism was being formed? What if the entire skip is a mind and the orbs are the equivalent of its neurons? More to the point what happens when it gains enough of itself to become aware?

Well done either way. My first up vote on the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You'll find the (out-of-universe) titles for SCPs on the series pages: Series I, Series II and Series III.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What is the name ò this SCP?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah this seems more like an example of "How To Build An Afterlife In The Heavens Without Remembering Space is Cold" more than some nefarious hell-torture scenario.

Bug fixing is the difference between Purgatory and Hell.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I totally love everything about this. The science, the poetry, the image, the excellent writing, the clever basic idea and its clever development (Any human corpse under SCP-2838 will dematerialise if left unobserved for more than 24 hours. A new SCP-2838-1 instance will then appear within SCP-2838, usually instantaneously. SCP-2838 was formerly designated AO-17508 in Foundation records, only entering containment when its effects were first observed in the aftermath of [REDACTED], near the beginning of the Tajikistan Civil War.. WOW. So much said in two sentences!)… everything.
And I SO like that Bronze Age people managed to do this, and we don't even know how it could possibly work!
I'm probably a bit twisted in my mind, but I don't understand the addendum as necessarily horrible. You know what my first idea was when I read it: 'These people feel warm when they are worshipped'. How overoptimistic can you get? ;>

I am SO glad I don't have to judge this contest: I have about 15 top top favourite articles, plus many others I love, and many other I like very much, etc. And I'm about half-way through the list! I couldn't possibly choose one…

Excellent job, minmin. Take this upvote, with a beautiful bouquet of flowers around it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Science attempts to understand a real and horribly fucked-up afterlife" is in danger of becoming a trope around here. Until then though, this is quite fertile ground for horror. Nicely done. I especially enjoyed the subversion of the cliched "stars as human souls" thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know, they might not be feeling the heat. Their average temperature is thousands of degrees above the environment. Maybe their internal temperature feels normal to them.

They're feeling the air around themselves. They feel cold.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dear god this is a good entry

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, I started reading what I thought was a beautiful, yet cliched, metaphor for humanity being made of dead stars.

Then I read the addendum and the footnote and now I… kinda want to cry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 51 cm…the average length of a newborn baby. Makes it even a little scarier. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Listen, all I can really say to this is "fuck, dude." I usually pride myself on, uh, better commentary than that. But… fuck, dude.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now you've made me think of Mufasa burning eternally. Take my +1, and my hatred.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this - the understated, clinical tone works very well, and the mystery is maintained nicely.

A nitpick: in "Specifically, regions of SCP-2838-1's head corresponding …" you're refering to SCP-2838-1 as a single entity, whereas in the rest of the piece you use "SCP-2838-1 instances".

In my headcannon, it's not certain that they're burning. Thermoreception areas are also highly stimulated from intense cold, and the surrounding environment is much colder than their surface temperatures. They might be freezing forever. Or the foundation might have mismapped their brains.

Definite +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah fuck that. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope, because of magic Foundation concealment protocols… waves hands

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Indeed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: On that note, would they be visible if one views them from above?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 2838 extends 12km above ground level, which I just realised is another way of saying that the highest 2838-1 instances are found 12km above ground level. For reference: 12km above ground level is just touching the tropopause , which is still below some clouds.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pros:

  • Good explanation of your SCP. It can be easily comprehended and you know where this is going.
  • I also like how you brought about the Bronze Age elements into the piece.

Cons:

  • It's a bit ambiguous as to how high are the 2838-1 instances. Are they reachable from within our atmosphere or are they in space?

Verdict: Upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Amigara Fault was a deliberate punishment. This is a Bronze Age society's unfortunate misunderstanding of their afterlife.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Specifically, regions of SCP-2838-1's head corresponding to the regions of the brain responsible for thermoreception appear to be highly stimulated.

JESUS FUCK

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is tantalizing, but I couldn't get it out of my head that it was The Enigma of Amigara Fault.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The addendum is, tonally and structurally, everything you want from the ending of a short-form article - mysterious, understated, and troubling. The anomaly is a small, but interesting and compelling idea with a great central image, and it's fleshed out enough to deliver the stinger without leaving it looking like a rough sketch. This is the sort of thing I hoped might come out of this contest, and the sort of thing I was worried I might not actually see. Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
We are all made of starstuff…

Nice sense of wonder from this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They're burning and feeling it forever… is what I get from this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has a good premise and it sparked a moderate amount of feels in me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Short contest entry! Thanks to all who reviewed it in chat, and image credits goes to user Hermitsmoores on Flickr.
(source: https://www.flickr.com/photos/hermitsmoores/9457788657/in/photostream/ )

Edit: I can't seem to upload the picture… Excuse the broken link while I try to get a better internet connection Σ(⊙▽⊙")
